columns.extraction_plan: Extract the number and percentage from Table 1, under the "All Patients (N = 1,305)" column, specifically in the "Darolutamide (n = 651)" sub-column, for the "ECOG PS, No. (%)", row "0".
Extract the number and percentage from Table 1, under the "All Patients (N = 1,305)" column, specifically in the "Placebo (n = 654)" sub-column, for the "ECOG PS, No. (%)", row "0".

Extract the number and percentage from Table 1, under the "All Patients (N = 1,305)" column, specifically in the "Darolutamide (n = 651)" sub-column, for the "ECOG PS, No. (%)", row "1". The table only reports ECOG PS 0 and 1, and the sum of these categories (plus 0 missing) accounts for all patients in the Darolutamide arm (466 + 185 = 651). This implies no patients had ECOG PS 2 or higher. Therefore, the value for PS 1-2 is equivalent to PS 1.

Extract the number and percentage from Table 1, under the "All Patients (N = 1,305)" column, specifically in the "Placebo (n = 654)" sub-column, for the "ECOG PS, No. (%)", row "1". The table only reports ECOG PS 0 and 1, and the sum of these categories (plus 2 missing) accounts for all patients in the Placebo arm (462 + 190 + 2 = 654). This implies no patients had ECOG PS 2 or higher. Therefore, the value for PS 1-2 is equivalent to PS 1.
